note_data.title: Reactivity of metals
keyPoints: The **reactivity series** is the order of metals arranged in decreasing order of their reactivity
**More reactive metals** displace less reactive metals from solutions of their compounds
Metal **displacement reactions** involve the more reactive metal taking the place of the less reactive metal
**Hydrogen** is included in the reactivity series to show which metals react with acids
Metals above hydrogen in the reactivity series **react with dilute acids** to produce hydrogen gas
Metals below hydrogen in the reactivity series **do not react with dilute acids**
The **ease of extraction** of metals from their ores depends on their position in the reactivity series
More reactive metals require **more energy** to extract from their compounds than less reactive metals
**Rusting of iron** can be prevented by coating with more reactive metals like zinc (galvanizing)

definitions.definition: The arrangement of metals in order of decreasing reactivity
A reaction where a more reactive metal takes the place of a less reactive metal in a compound
Coating iron with zinc to prevent rusting
The removal of oxygen from a compound or the gain of electrons

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
State what will happen and give a reason based on reactivity

modelAnswer.question: Zinc powder is added to copper sulfate solution. State what you would observe and explain why this reaction occurs.
modelAnswer.answer: The blue color of copper sulfate solution disappears and a brown/reddish solid forms. This reaction occurs because zinc is more reactive than copper, so zinc displaces copper from copper sulfate solution.
markBreakdown: Mark 3: Zinc is more reactive than copper / displacement occurs

commonMistakes: Never say 'stronger metal' — always say 'more reactive metal'
Students often confuse the direction of displacement — remember more reactive displaces less reactive
Don't write 'metals below hydrogen react with acids' — they do NOT react
Never say 'zinc protects iron by covering it' — zinc protects because it is more reactive and gets oxidized first
Students forget to include observations like color changes in displacement reactions
examinerTips: Reactivity series questions appear in almost every Paper 2
Always learn the order: Potassium, Sodium, Calcium, Magnesium, Aluminum, Zinc, Iron, Lead, Hydrogen, Copper, Silver, Gold
Use the mnemonic: Please Stop Calling Me A Zebra In Leeds Hospital Copper Silver Gold
For displacement reactions, always state the observation AND the explanation
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Reactivity series arranged vertically with most reactive (potassium) at top and least reactive (gold) at bottom, with hydrogen positioned between lead and copper
